purpose: settings.parallel_computing=true AND fp_version=true on the exact parallel-computing-on program: the dispatch at program.py:42-46 checks _parallel_computing before _fp_version, so InterpretationParallel wins and the fp kernel is never constructed. Proven behaviorally, not just by readback: every reasoning probe digest matched parallel-computing-on's at authoring, while trace-node and nodes-popular did NOT match fp-version-on's banked artifacts (the fp kernel's fp-counter values, event order, duplicated atom-trace groundings, and last-step frame row order differ on identical inputs, per the fp-version-on case record; trace-edge and time match all engines vacuously — empty edge trace, get_time 3) — the interaction-fp-precedence class (setting:parallel_computing) and interaction-parallel-precedence class (setting:fp_version) in one case. Both knob readbacks return true: precedence is a dispatch fact, not a setting mutation.
provenance: spine case against docs/surface.md setting:parallel_computing interaction-fp-precedence and setting:fp_version interaction-parallel-precedence; hello-world program with both engine-selection knobs set
